title: Module 1 – Weeks 1-2: Foundations
introduction: This module focuses on the initial steps of myofunctional therapy. We'll start with active tongue exercises, sensory input, and your baseline breathing assessment.
overview: Focus: Active exercises for tongue control, sensory stimulation, and establishing a baseline for your breathing health.
objectives: Develop sensory awareness of the tongue and palate

exercises.name: Clicks and Tick-Tocks
exercises.description: Clicks (1 min): Suction tongue to roof for 3 sec, then click while keeping cheeks back. Tick-Tocks (1 min): Fast clicking sounds moving lips from smile to pursed.

exercises.name: Brushing
exercises.description: Stick out tongue and brush full tongue 5 times. Brush 'the spot' area 5 times. Repeat for 1 minute total.

exercises.name: Tongue Trace
exercises.description: Tip of tongue against 'the spot' and trace back along the palate in a straight line as far as possible.

exercises.name: BOLT Test
exercises.description: Measure your baseline breathing health. Record the time in seconds before the first sign of 'air hunger'.

exercises.name: Tongue Tip – One Elastic Hold
exercises.description: Place elastic on the tip of the tongue and hold it to 'the spot' for 5 minutes.

title: Week 2 – Tongue Strength & Cheek Control
introduction: Now that you've found your spot, it's time to make your tongue stronger while keeping your cheeks relaxed. Strong tongue muscles mean your airway stays open and your teeth have better support. Cheeks and lips sometimes 'help' too much, and that creates strain — so we'll train them to stay relaxed. This week is about control, not force. The more balanced your muscles are, the easier breathing and swallowing become.
overview: Let's build a stronger tongue while keeping your cheeks relaxed. Small corrections here make a big difference later.

exercises.name: Tongue Press
exercises.description: Press your tongue flat against the roof of your mouth for 3 seconds. Repeat 10 times.

exercises.name: Mirror Check
exercises.description: Repeat in front of a mirror, ensuring your cheeks stay relaxed.

title: Module 2 – Weeks 3-4: Suction & Breathing
introduction: In this module, we advance to suction strength and deep breathing techniques. Mastering these is crucial for airway stability.
overview: Focus: Lingual palatal suction, tongue points, and diaphragmatic breathing.

exercises.name: Lingual Palatal Suction
exercises.description: Suction tongue to roof and hold for as long as possible (10s to 1 min). Use a bite block if needed.

exercises.name: Tongue Points (Left and Right)
exercises.description: With neutral face, touch the smallest point of the tip of tongue to the corner of the mouth. Repeat for 2 minutes each side.

exercises.name: Cheek Puffs
exercises.description: Fill mouth with air and move to right, left, top lip, and bottom lip holding for 5 seconds each.

exercises.name: Belly/Diaphragm Breathing
exercises.description: Lie on back, hands on stomach and chest. Breathe into your belly so only your stomach moves.

exercises.name: Middle of Tongue – One Elastic Hold
exercises.description: Place elastic on the middle of the tongue and hold it to the roof of the mouth for 5 minutes.

title: Week 4 – Tongue Lift Challenge
introduction: This week is about lifting your tongue smoothly and evenly. A full tongue lift keeps your palate supported and gives stability to your whole oral posture. Think of it as strength training for your airway — the stronger your tongue, the clearer your breathing path. Don't worry if it feels tricky at first; control comes with practice. Every rep is an investment in easier breathing and better sleep.
overview: Your tongue is stronger now! Let's practice lifting smoothly and evenly.

exercises.name: Full Tongue Lift
exercises.description: Press the entire tongue against the palate for 5 seconds, repeat 10 times.

exercises.name: Cheek Control
exercises.description: Watch in a mirror to keep cheeks from moving.

title: Module 3 – Weeks 5-6: Speech & Lip Trace
introduction: This module introduces sound patterns and more complex lip/tongue coordination drills.
overview: Focus: K sounds, tongue in cheek, and circular lip movements.
objectives: Improve soft palate control with K sounds

exercises.name: Tongue in Cheek
exercises.description: Push tongue into cheek for 5 seconds without jaw shift. Keep face relaxed. 1 minute 2x daily.

exercises.name: K Sounds (Kuh, Guh, Unk)
exercises.description: Make precise Kuh sounds with mouth open. Focus on back of tongue touching the soft palate.

exercises.name: Lip Trace
exercises.description: With lips closed, trace circles around the inside of the upper and lower lips. 1 minute 2x daily.

exercises.name: 4-7-8 Breathing Pattern
exercises.description: Inhale 4s, Hold 7s, Exhale 8s. Repeat for 5 minutes.

exercises.name: Middle of Tongue – One Elastic Hold
exercises.description: Increased duration: Hold elastic on middle of tongue for 10 minutes.

title: Week 6 – Swallow Training Basics
introduction: This week we retrain your swallow. Many people push with their lips, cheeks, or even their chin when they swallow — but the healthiest swallow is led by the tongue. By keeping your tongue on the spot and letting your throat do the work, you create a cleaner, more efficient swallow. This reduces strain on facial muscles and supports long-term oral health. Each correct swallow is one step closer to lasting change.
overview: Now let's retrain your swallow — smooth, no cheek or chin strain.

exercises.name: Spot Swallow
exercises.description: Keep tongue on the spot while swallowing, feel throat muscles (not chin) working.

exercises.name: Water Sips
exercises.description: Take small sips, repeat correct swallow each time.

title: Week 7 – Jaw & Cheek Awareness
introduction: Your cheeks and jaw love to 'help out,' but this week we're teaching them to stay calm. Too much movement in those areas makes swallowing less efficient and can strain your bite. By practicing quiet, controlled swallows, you'll train your tongue to do the heavy lifting. Over time, this saves energy and reduces tension. Imagine swallowing smoothly without even thinking — that's where you're headed.
overview: Less jaw movement, more tongue control. Focus on quiet, efficient swallows.

exercises.name: Cheek Relax
exercises.description: Press tongue up while teeth stay apart and cheeks soft.

exercises.name: Quiet Swallow
exercises.description: Swallow in front of mirror; watch for no chin or jaw movement.

title: Week 8 – Breathing Under Effort
introduction: Breathing is more than just air — it's energy for your whole body. This week challenges you to maintain nose breathing even when your body is moving or working harder. Keeping your mouth closed during activity strengthens your airway and builds endurance. You'll notice calmer breathing, more stamina, and even better recovery after exercise. Think of this as fitness training for your breathing system.
overview: Maintain nasal breathing even with mild exertion. A key endurance skill.

exercises.name: Walking Nose Breathing
exercises.description: Walk 5 minutes breathing only through your nose, lips sealed.

exercises.name: BOLT Practice
exercises.description: Measure BOLT score once per day, note changes.

title: Post-Op Days 1–7: Initial Healing & Gentle Mobility
introduction: The first week after your procedure is all about healing and very gentle movement. Do not force any stretches during this time. The goal is simply to keep the area clean and maintain light mobility to prevent the tissue from healing too tightly. Focus on rest and follow the post-op care instructions provided by your surgeon.
overview: Focus: Wound healing and very gentle tongue mobility (up/down/side-to-side).

exercises.name: Gentle Tongue Lift (Days 1-3)
exercises.description: Slowly and gently lift the tip of your tongue toward the roof of your mouth. Stop if you feel any significant discomfort. Repeat 5 times, 3x daily.

exercises.name: Gentle Side Traces (Days 1-3)
exercises.description: Slowly move your tongue toward the corners of your mouth. Keep it very gentle. 5 times per side, 3x daily.

exercises.name: Lingual Palatal Suction (Days 4-7)
exercises.description: If comfortable, begin light suction to the roof of the mouth. Hold for 3-5 seconds. Repeat 5 times.

exercises.name: Floor of Mouth Massage (Days 4-7)
exercises.description: Using clean hands, gently massage the floor of the mouth under the tongue to encourage blood flow and healing.

title: Post-Op Days 8–14: Resuming Routine & Mobility
introduction: By the second week, initial healing is typically well underway. We now begin to re-introduce more structured stretches and exercises to ensure optimal mobility. Mouth taping is also introduced to support nasal breathing during sleep, which is critical for long-term recovery and oral health.
overview: Focus: Resuming active exercises, introducing surgical stretches, and nighttime mouth taping.

exercises.name: Tongue Stretch (Post-Op)
exercises.description: Slowly extend your tongue toward the spot and then further back along the palate. Hold for 5 seconds. Repeat 10 times.

exercises.name: Forklift Stretch
exercises.description: Place two fingers under the sides of your tongue and gently lift it straight up toward the roof of the mouth. Hold the stretch for 5-10 seconds.

exercises.name: Mouth Taping (Nighttime)
exercises.description: Apply a small piece of medical tape vertically across the center of your lips before sleep to ensure you breathe through your nose all night.

exercises.name: Suction Hold (Extended)
exercises.description: Perform the Lingual Palatal Suction and hold for as long as comfortable, aiming for 15-20 seconds.

title: Module 5 – Weeks 11-12: Linking Habits
introduction: Your exercises shouldn't just live in practice time — they should follow you into everyday life. This week is about integrating your skills during meals, conversations, and casual moments. The more often you practice in real life, the faster they become natural habits. Imagine sitting at dinner, breathing calmly and swallowing smoothly without thinking — that's the goal.
overview: Bring your tongue posture and breathing into daily life moments.

exercises.name: Meal Practice
exercises.description: During meals, pause to check lip seal and proper swallow.

exercises.name: Conversation Breathing
exercises.description: Between sentences, breathe calmly through your nose.

title: Module 5 – Completion: Midpoint Check
introduction: Congratulations, you've reached the halfway point! This week is about reviewing your progress and seeing how far you've come. Recording your posture and swallow now gives you proof of the improvements you've made. Even small wins — calmer breathing, fewer open-mouth moments — are worth celebrating. Remember, the effort you've put in is laying the foundation for lasting health.
overview: You're halfway! Time for a checkpoint — review posture, breathing, and swallowing.

exercises.name: Posture Scan
exercises.description: Check resting tongue, lips, and breathing every hour today.

exercises.name: Midpoint Video
exercises.description: Record yourself doing tongue-to-spot hold and a correct swallow.

title: Module 6 – Weeks 13-14: Adding Strength
introduction: It's time to add intensity. Your tongue is stronger now, so let's push it further. Stronger holds and longer times make your airway more stable and your posture more reliable. Think of this as strength training for your mouth and airway. The more consistent you are, the easier these skills will feel in daily life.
overview: We'll add intensity — stronger holds, longer times.

exercises.name: Power Hold
exercises.description: Press tongue strongly against palate for 7 seconds, 10 reps.

exercises.name: Relaxation Reset
exercises.description: After holds, consciously relax cheeks and jaw.

title: Module 6 – Completion: Speaking & Breathing
introduction: Speaking and breathing often get out of sync — but not anymore. This week you'll learn how to maintain good tongue posture and nasal breathing even when you talk. This helps you speak more clearly, avoid strain, and stay energized. These small adjustments can improve confidence and make conversations feel easier.
overview: Apply your skills during speech and conversation.

exercises.name: Read Aloud Drill
exercises.description: Read aloud for 5 minutes, pause with lips closed, breathe through nose.

exercises.name: Speech Awareness
exercises.description: Notice tongue rest position when not speaking.

title: Module 7 – Weeks 15-16: Endurance Challenge
introduction: Endurance is the theme again. Long holds and consistent breathing create the muscle memory you need for automatic posture. Just like training for a sport, endurance sets you apart. You're building habits that last through the day, not just during practice. The stronger your endurance, the less you'll have to think about it.
overview: Push stamina — longer holds, consistent breathing.

exercises.name: Long Hold
exercises.description: Hold tongue suction for 20 seconds, 5 times.

exercises.name: Movement + Nose Breathing
exercises.description: Do light exercise (stairs/walk) while keeping nose breathing.

title: Module 7 – Completion: Chew & Swallow Refinement
introduction: This week is about chewing and swallowing refinement. Eating is something we all do every day, and practicing proper mechanics here reinforces everything you've learned. When your chew and swallow are efficient, digestion improves and muscles stay balanced. It may seem small, but this is one of the most practical skills you'll ever master.
overview: Focus on chewing and swallowing with control.

exercises.name: Chewing Practice
exercises.description: Chew food slowly, tongue stays up, lips together.

exercises.name: Correct Swallow Drill
exercises.description: 10 practice swallows, tongue on spot, no cheek movement.

title: Module 8 – Weeks 17-18: Breathing Endurance
introduction: Breathing endurance is your focus now. By training yourself to breathe calmly through your nose for longer, you're building a stronger airway and a more resilient body. Nose breathing even during activity reduces fatigue and supports better sleep. Think of this as building the stamina to keep your body supplied with energy all day long.
overview: Build breath endurance and calm rhythm.

exercises.name: Nose Walk
exercises.description: Walk 10 minutes nose-only breathing, lips sealed.

exercises.name: Breath Holds
exercises.description: Practice gentle holds and measure BOLT once daily.

title: Module 8 – Completion: Swallow Integration
introduction: This week we want your correct swallow to feel automatic. By now, you've practiced enough that it should start to happen without effort. The goal is for your body to choose the right motion naturally. Automatic, efficient swallows protect your teeth, jaw, and airway every day — without you needing to think about it.
overview: Automatic swallows with no effort.

exercises.name: Sip & Swallow
exercises.description: Drink 10 sips, swallow correctly each time.

exercises.name: Daily Check
exercises.description: Notice each swallow throughout day, correct if off.

title: Module 9 – Weeks 19-20: Speech & Smile
introduction: Smiles and conversations — that's where your skills shine this week. You'll practice keeping posture and breathing steady even while talking and smiling. This creates confidence and consistency. When your habits stay strong even in social settings, you know the work is paying off.
overview: Blend tongue posture with natural speech and smiling.

exercises.name: Smile Practice
exercises.description: Smile naturally, tongue stays on spot.

exercises.name: Conversation Drill
exercises.description: In casual talk, keep lips sealed between sentences.

title: Module 9 – Completion: Challenge Week
introduction: This is a challenge week. You'll test yourself with endurance, posture, and breathing under pressure. Think of it like a personal milestone — a way to measure everything you've built so far. The stronger you finish this week, the more ready you'll be for the final stretch.
overview: Test endurance, posture, and breathing under stress.

exercises.name: Endurance Hold
exercises.description: Hold suction for 30 seconds, repeat 3 times.

exercises.name: Exercise Nose Breathing
exercises.description: Try jogging/walking briskly nose-only for 5 minutes.

title: Module 10 – Weeks 21-22: Everyday Habits
introduction: This week is about making your skills part of everyday life. Every activity — school, work, meals, conversations — becomes a chance to reinforce your posture and breathing. The more natural it feels, the closer you are to making it permanent. You're not just practicing now; you're living it.
overview: Your skills should feel automatic now. Bring them into every moment.

exercises.name: Posture Scan
exercises.description: Every hour, check tongue up, lips closed, nose breathing.

exercises.name: Daily Life Drill
exercises.description: At work/school, notice posture and breathing often.

title: Module 10 – Completion: Maintenance Focus
introduction: Refinement continues. This week you'll polish your habits so they feel effortless. Smooth swallows, steady posture, calm breathing — they should all start blending into daily life. This is where the hard work transforms into an easy rhythm.
overview: Fine-tune — smoother, easier, natural.

exercises.name: Smooth Swallows
exercises.description: Swallow 10 times with no visible movement.

exercises.name: Relaxed Holds
exercises.description: Hold tongue posture for 1 minute comfortably.

title: Module 11 – Weeks 23-24: Final Review
introduction: Almost there! This is your practice week before the final celebration. Use this time to polish your skills and record yourself in action. Think of it like rehearsing for graduation. You're preparing to show off the progress you've earned.
overview: One more week to polish everything.

exercises.name: Combo Drill
exercises.description: Tongue up, lips closed, nose breathing during reading, smiling, speaking.

exercises.name: Pre-Final Video
exercises.description: Practice your final video performance.

title: Module 11 – Completion: Graduation & Beyond
introduction: Congratulations — you've reached the finish line! This is your chance to showcase everything you've learned and celebrate the transformation. You started with small steps, and now you have stronger posture, better breathing, and healthier habits. The effort you've put in has paid off. This is not the end — it's the beginning of a healthier, more confident you.
overview: This week celebrates your progress — posture, breathing, and confidence are now yours.

exercises.name: Final Combo
exercises.description: Read aloud, smile, breathe calmly — all skills together.

exercises.name: Final Video
exercises.description: Record your progress video and celebrate your success.
